trans-unit.source: We have sent an email to reset your password in your inbox.
Password must contain an uppercase letter, a number and a special character.
Overview of features and procedures on the platform.
An exercise is a set of activities that participants complete during a session.
Open the Exercises section in the main menu and click to create a new exercise.
Enter the name of the exercise.
Insert quizzes, guess, put in order, or match up.
The exercise is saved and ready for use in sessions.
How to create and start a session
A session is a running instance of an exercise in which participants join and complete prepared activities in real time.
Open the list of exercises and select the one you want to start in the session.
Select a mode (Classic, Hot Potato, Self-study) and other parameters.
Upon launch, a unique code is generated for participants.
Participants join using the generated code.
The session host will provide you with the code.
Enter your nickname for session identification.
Enter the session by clicking the join button.
An activity is a task within an exercise to which participants respond.
At a certain point in the session, an activity (e.g., a quiz, guess or match up) will appear for you to complete.

In each activity, you have two main actions available: Confirm answer – sends the final answer, which is then evaluated.Save state – saves the draft response to the server, so it can be reloaded later or shared within the team.
After confirming the answer, the system waits for the other participants. Once everyone has finished, the results and feedback are displayed.
The answer is the confirmed solution for the given activity.
The mode determines how the session proceeds. It influences the rules, timing, and method of evaluating activities within the exercise.
Join a session using a code and participate in real-time activities and learning modes.
Enter the name of the new exercise.
This exercise does not yet contain any activities.
You haven't created any exercises yet.
You do not have any active sessions yet.
Customize the app's language and appearance according to your preferences.
To enhance security, we recommend changing your password regularly.
The password must contain an uppercase letter, a number, and a special character.
This action cannot be undone. All your data will be permanently deleted.
You do not have any completed sessions yet.
Distribution of response success rates across groups
Average percentage accuracy by activity type
Average response success rate by activity type
History of responses to the given activity
Select the type of activity you want to create for this exercise.
Create the first activity for this exercise.
Enter the session code and your nickname.
Enter the name of the new team
Select the team you want to join.
No team has been created yet. You can close the dialog and try again later.
You are waiting for the session to start.
groups Joined the team: Without a team
The next activity will begin in a moment.
The next activity will start in a moment.
The others are still responding to the activity.
A hot potato is ready in no time.
The potato is heating up. The next round is about to start any moment.
Someone else is currently holding the hot potato.
You don't play for the rest of the game.
You won as the last player.
Why is there night and day?
Selecting the correct answer from the options
Selection of one or more answers for quick knowledge verification
Text-based responses where the participant fills in or guesses the correct answer.
Arranging items in the correct order
Arranging items in the correct order.
Standard walkthrough of the exercise by the host
The activities take place in a pre-set order, and each has a time limit. Upon completion of a round, participants are shown the results and feedback.
Rapid player rotation under timer pressure
In each round, "potatoes"—each containing an activity—are assigned to the active players. When time runs out, the slowest players are eliminated, and the game continues until a single winner remains.
Self-paced learning with question selection based on a chosen algorithm.

Participants progress at their own pace. The system adaptively selects activities based on their performance: activities are repeated more frequently if errors occur often, and less frequently if the participant is successful. The host monitors the results in real time.
Duration of a single game round
Determines how long one round lasts.
Determines the method by which questions are selected for players.
Adaptive selection of activities based on knowledge
Adaptive selection of activities based on performance
It sets the scoring rules for the game.
Each participant works independently and completes the activities on their own. Answers are not shared, and each participant is evaluated separately.
Multiple participants collaborate in a team.

Participants work in groups to collaboratively create a single shared response. Each team member can add to or edit the draft response and share its status. Any team member can then confirm the final response.
